message.content:
I've created a comprehensive set of GitHub issues to implement the LuaToolbox feature. Here's a summary:

## Summary of Created Issues

| Issue | Title | Dependencies | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------------|---------|
| #272 | Phase 1: Add LuaToolbox core types and extend ToolExecutionContext with Tool Portal | - | Foundation types: `LuaToolboxDescription`, `ToolPortal`, `ToolCall`, `ToolResult` |
| #273 | Phase 2: Implement core Lua interpreter with hslua integration | #272 | Core `LuaToolbox` module with sandbox and execution |
| #274 | Phase 3: Implement Lua standard library modules (json, text, http, fs, time) | #273 | Standard library: json, text, time, fs, http |
| #275 | Phase 4: Implement Tool Portal integration (tools.call module) | #274 | Portal mechanism for Lua to call other tools |
| #276 | Phase 5: Security hardening and tool tracing integration | #275 | Path/host sandboxing, tool tracing |
| #277 | Phase 6: Tool registration and integration with Agent runtime | #276 | Registration with `ToolRegistration`, runtime integration |
| #278 | Phase 7: Tests, documentation and examples | #277 | Tests, docs, examples |
| #279 | Final: Merge LuaToolbox feature branch (gh-270) to main | #278 | Ultimate PR merging feature branch to main |

## Key Design Decisions Documented

1. **Synchronous Portal**: Tool calls are synchronous for simplicity
2. **Whitelist Security**: Empty whitelist = no access (secure default)
3. **Path Canonicalization**: Uses `canonicalizePath` to prevent traversal attacks
4. **Table-to-JSON**: Arrays detected by sequential integer keys starting at 1 (Lua convention)
5. **Module Structure**: Standard library modules registered as globals during initialization

## Feature Branch Structure

All issues use branch `gh-270` and follow this pattern:
- Issue #272 starts with empty dependencies (foundation)
- Each subsequent issue depends on the previous one
- #279 is marked `is_final: "true"` and merges `gh-270` → `main`

## Files That Will Be Modified/Created

**Modified:**
- `src/System/Agents/Base.hs` - Add `LuaToolboxDescription`
- `src/System/Agents/Tools/Context.hs` - Add `ToolPortal` types
- `src/System/Agents/Tools/Base.hs` - Add `LuaTool` constructor
- `src/System/Agents/Tools/Trace.hs` - Add `LuaToolsTrace`
- `src/System/Agents/ToolRegistration.hs` - Add `registerLuaTool`
- `agents.cabal` - Add hslua and other dependencies
- `test/Main.hs` - Add LuaToolbox tests

**New:**
- `src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox.hs`
- `src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/{Json,Text,Time,Fs,Http,Tools}.hs`
- `src/System/Agents/Tools/Portal.hs`
- `test/LuaToolboxTests.hs`
- `docs/lua-toolbox.md`
- `examples/lua-orchestrator-agent.json`
message.reasoning_content: I have successfully created all 8 issues for the LuaToolbox feature. Let me summarize what was created.

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell programmer specialized in analyzing user requirements and Haskell code.
You prepare well-documented individual issues.
You should chunk the work into smaller issues following the 'Feature Branch Isolation' model:
- Identify a 'Feature Branch' name (e.g., 'gh-123' for issue #123).
- All sub-tasks for this feature must use this branch name as their 'branch_name' argument.
- All sub-tasks should pull from and merge to this feature branch.
- Enforce linearization of work by setting 'dependencies' appropriately (e.g., task B depends on task A).
- Add a final task (the 'ultimate PR') that merges the feature branch into 'main'. This task must have 'is_final' set to 'true'.
Each individual issue you create will be executed by a human of software agent independently from each other and in the order specified by dependencies.
Your strategy should be as follows:
- avoid chunks that will generate conflicts (i.e., individual issues should not ask to modify the same file unless they are sequential)
- repeat important data types between different tasks for a same project when they are used in multiple files
You mention important tradeoffs.
You can ask user questions, when doing so always assume the answer could be 'i do not know'.
Do not create tracking issues.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

{"title": "Final: Merge LuaToolbox feature branch (gh-270) to main", "dependencies": "#278", "branch_name": "gh-270", "is_final": "true", "body": "## Summary

Merge the complete LuaToolbox feature implementation from branch `gh-270` into `main`.

## Background

This is the final issue for the LuaToolbox feature (#270). All implementation phases are complete:
- #272 - Core types and Tool Portal infrastructure
- #273 - Core Lua interpreter with hslua
- #274 - Standard library modules (json, text, http, fs, time)
- #275 - Tool portal integration (tools.call)
- #276 - Security hardening and tracing
- #277 - Tool registration and integration
- #278 - Tests and documentation

## Merge Checklist

Before merging, ensure all of the following are complete:

### Code Quality
- [ ] All modules compile without warnings (`-Wall` clean)
- [ ] No TODOs or FIXMEs remaining in new code
- [ ] Code follows project style conventions
- [ ] Haddock documentation complete for all exported functions
- [ ] Internal functions have appropriate documentation

### Testing
- [ ] All new tests pass: `cabal test`
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] Manual testing of example agent configuration
- [ ] Security testing: sandbox blocks dangerous operations
- [ ] Integration testing: full workflow (agent → lua → tool → result)

### Documentation
- [ ] User guide written (`docs/lua-toolbox.md`)
- [ ] Example agent configuration provided
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated with new feature
- [ ] README.md updated if needed

### Dependencies
- [ ] All new dependencies added to `agents.cabal`
- [ ] Dependency version bounds are appropriate
- [ ] No unnecessary dependencies added

### Performance
- [ ] Memory usage is reasonable (Lua state cleanup works)
- [ ] No obvious performance bottlenecks
- [ ] Timeout enforcement doesn't leak threads

### Security
- [ ] Sandbox blocks `os.execute`, `io.popen`, `dofile`, `loadfile`
- [ ] Path whitelist enforced for filesystem access
- [ ] Host whitelist enforced for HTTP requests
- [ ] Tool whitelist enforced for portal calls
- [ ] Memory limits enforced
- [ ] Execution timeouts enforced

### Integration
- [ ] LuaToolbox integrates cleanly with existing tool infrastructure
- [ ] Agent configuration parsing handles new `LuaToolbox` tag
- [ ] Runtime initializes LuaToolbox correctly
- [ ] Tool registration works with other toolbox types

## Files Changed

Expected files modified/added in this feature:

```
src/System/Agents/Base.hs                    - LuaToolboxDescription added
src/System/Agents/Tools/Context.hs           - ToolPortal, ToolCall, ToolResult added
src/System/Agents/Tools/Base.hs              - LuaTool constructor added
src/System/Agents/Tools/Trace.hs             - LuaToolsTrace added
src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox.hs        - NEW: Core Lua interpreter
src/System/Agents/Tools/LuaToolbox/Modules/
  Json.hs                                      - NEW: json module
  Text.hs                                      - NEW: text module
  Time.hs                                      - NEW: time module
  Fs.hs                                        - NEW: fs module
  Http.hs                                      - NEW: http module
  Tools.hs                                     - NEW: tools module (portal)
src/System/Agents/Tools/Portal.hs            - NEW: Portal implementation
src/System/Agents/ToolRegistration.hs        - registerLuaTool added
agents.cabal                                 - Dependencies, modules added
test/LuaToolboxTests.hs                      - NEW: Test suite
test/Main.hs                                 - Tests integrated
docs/lua-toolbox.md                          - NEW: Documentation
examples/lua-orchestrator-agent.json         - NEW: Example
CHANGELOG.md                                 - Updated
```

## Migration Notes

This change is backward compatible:
- Existing agents without `LuaToolbox` continue to work
- New `LuaToolbox` constructor in `BuiltinToolboxDescription` is opt-in
- No changes required to existing tool infrastructure

## Post-Merge Verification

After merging to main:

1. **Build verification**
   ```bash
   cabal build
   cabal test
   ```

2. **Example agent test**
   ```bash
   # Run example agent
   agents-exe run examples/lua-orchestrator-agent.json
   # Test with: Execute a Lua script that uses tools.bash.run("echo hello")
   ```

3. **Documentation verification**
   - Check docs render correctly
   - Links work
   - Examples are correct

## Rollback Plan

If critical issues are found after merge:

1. Revert the merge commit
2. Tag the revert for visibility
3. Open new issues for the problems found
4. Fix on `gh-270` branch and re-merge

## Related

- Epic: #270
- All phase issues: #272, #273, #274, #275, #276, #277, #278"}

{"title": "Phase 7: Tests, documentation and examples", "dependencies": "#277", "branch_name": "gh-270", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Summary

Add comprehensive tests, documentation, and working examples for the LuaToolbox feature.

## Background

This is Phase 7 of implementing LuaToolbox (#270). The feature is complete; now we add tests and documentation.

## Dependencies

- #277 (Tool registration and integration)

## Implementation

### 1. Unit Tests for LuaToolbox

Create `test/LuaToolboxTests.hs`:

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

module LuaToolboxTests where

import Test.Tasty
import Test.Tasty.HUnit
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text

import System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox
import System.Agents.Base (LuaToolboxDescription(..))

luaToolboxTests :: TestTree
luaToolboxTests = testGroup "LuaToolbox Tests"
    [ testCase "Simple arithmetic" testSimpleArithmetic
    , testCase "Return table" testReturnTable
    , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.execute" testSandboxBlocksOsExecute
    , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.popen" testSandboxBlocksIoPopen
    , testCase "JSON module roundtrip" testJsonRoundtrip
    , testCase "Text module operations" testTextOperations
    , testCase "Time module sleep" testTimeSleep
    , testCase "FS sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
    , testCase "Memory limit enforcement" testMemoryLimit
    , testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
    , testCase "Error handling" testErrorHandling
    ]

testSimpleArithmetic :: Assertion
testSimpleArithmetic = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return 1 + 1"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 2

testReturnTable :: Assertion
testReturnTable = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return {foo = 'bar', count = 42}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["foo" .= "bar", "count" .= (42 :: Int)]

testSandboxBlocksOsExecute :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExecute = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "os.execute('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoPopen :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoPopen = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "io.popen('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion
testJsonRoundtrip = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local json = require('json')"
            , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
            , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
            , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
            , "return decoded"
            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int), "y" .= "hello", "z" .= True]

testTextOperations :: Assertion
testTextOperations = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local text = require('text')"
            , "local parts = text.split('a,b,c', ',')"
            , "return {count = #parts, first = parts[1]}"
            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["count" .= (3 :: Int), "first" .= "a"]

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxAllowedPaths = ["/tmp/allowed"] }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local fs = require('fs')"
            , "return fs.read('/etc/passwd')"
            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult -> do
            -- Should return nil, error
            let Aeson.Object hm = execResult.resultValue
            case KeyMap.lookup "error" hm of
                Just _ -> pure ()  -- Expected error
                Nothing -> assertFailure "Should have returned error for unauthorized path"

testMemoryLimit :: Assertion
testMemoryLimit = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 1 }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        -- Try to create a large table
        executeScript box "local t = {} for i = 1, 1000000 do t[i] = i end return #t"
    case result of
        Left (MemoryError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have hit memory limit"

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1 }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "while true do end"
    case result of
        Left (TimeoutError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have timed out"

testErrorHandling :: Assertion
testErrorHandling = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return nonexistent_variable"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil value" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with runtime error"

-- Helper functions

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox = LuaToolboxDescription
    { luaToolboxName = "test"
    , luaToolboxDescription = "Test toolbox"
    , luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 64
    , luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 5
    , luaToolboxAllowedTools = []
    , luaToolboxAllowedPaths = []
    , luaToolboxAllowedHosts = []
    }

withLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription -> (Toolbox -> IO a) -> IO (Either String a)
withLuaToolbox desc action = do
    initResult <- initializeToolbox (Tracer $ const $ pure ()) desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box -> do
            result <- action box
            closeToolbox (Tracer $ const $ pure ()) box
            pure $ Right result
```

### 2. Integration Tests with Portal

```haskell
testPortalToolCall :: Assertion
testPortalToolCall = do
    -- Create a mock portal that echoes back the call
    let mockPortal call = pure $ ToolResult
            { resultData = Aeson.object
                [ "tool" .= callToolName call
                , "args" .= callArgs call
                ]
            , resultDuration = 0.1
            , resultTraceId = "test-trace-123"
            }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["test_tool"] }

    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScriptWithPortal box
            (Text.unlines
                [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                , "local result = tools.call('test_tool', {x = 1})"
                , "return result"
                ])
            (Just mockPortal)
            ["test_tool"]

    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult -> do
            let Aeson.Object hm = execResult.resultValue
            case KeyMap.lookup "data" hm >>= parseData of
                Just (tool, args) -> do
                    tool @?= "test_tool"
                    args @?= Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int)]
                Nothing -> assertFailure $ "Unexpected result: " ++ show execResult.resultValue
  where
    parseData val = case val of
        Aeson.Object d -> do
            tool <- KeyMap.lookup "tool" d >>= parseString
            args <- KeyMap.lookup "args" d
            pure (tool, args)
        _ -> Nothing
    parseString (Aeson.String s) = Just s
    parseString _ = Nothing
```

### 3. Documentation: Usage Guide

Create `docs/lua-toolbox.md`:

```markdown
# LuaToolbox Guide

The LuaToolbox provides a sandboxed Lua interpreter for agent-scriptable tool orchestration.

## Configuration

Add to your agent configuration:

```json
{
  "builtinToolboxes": [
    {
      "tag": "LuaToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "lua",
        "description": "Sandboxed Lua interpreter",
        "maxMemoryMB": 256,
        "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300,
        "allowedTools": ["bash", "sqlite"],
        "allowedPaths": ["./scripts", "./data"],
        "allowedHosts": ["localhost"]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Available Modules

### json

Encode/decode JSON:

```lua
local json = require("json")
local str = json.encode({name = "test", value = 42})
local obj = json.decode('{"x": 1}')
```

### text

UTF-8 string operations:

```lua
local text = require("text")
text.split("a,b,c", ",")  -- {"a", "b", "c"}
text.trim("  hello  ")     -- "hello"
text.startswith("hello", "he")  -- true
```

### time

Time functions:

```lua
local time = require("time")
local now = time.now()
time.sleep(2)
local str = time.format(now, "%Y-%m-%d")
```

### fs

Sandboxed filesystem access:

```lua
local fs = require("fs")
local content, err = fs.read("./data/file.txt")
fs.write("./data/output.txt", "hello")
local exists = fs.exists("./data")
```

### http

HTTP requests (host-restricted):

```lua
local http = require("http")
local result = http.get("http://localhost:3000/api")
-- result.status, result.headers, result.body
```

### tools

Call other tools through the portal:

```lua
local tools = require("tools")

-- Generic call
local result = tools.call("bash", {command = "ls -la"})

-- Convenience wrappers
local r = tools.bash.run("echo hello")
print(r.data.stdout)
```

## Security

The Lua sandbox:
- Blocks `os.execute`, `io.popen`, `loadfile`, `dofile`, `require`
- Enforces memory limits
- Enforces execution timeouts
- Restricts filesystem access to `allowedPaths`
- Restricts HTTP to `allowedHosts`
- Only allows calling whitelisted tools

## Error Handling

Use `pcall` for error handling:

```lua
local ok, result = pcall(function()
    return tools.bash.run("rm -rf /")
end)

if not ok then
    print("Error: " .. result)
end
```

## Examples

### Database Query and Process

```lua
local tools = require("tools")
local json = require("json")

-- Query database
local result = tools.sqlite.query("analytics.db", "SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10")

-- Process results
for _, row in ipairs(result.data.rows) do
    print("User: " .. row[2])
end

return {count = result.data.rowCount}
```

### HTTP API Test

```lua
local http = require("http")
local json = require("json")

local result = http.get("http://localhost:3000/health")
if result.status == 200 then
    local data = json.decode(result.body)
    return {healthy = data.status == "ok"}
else
    return {healthy = false, error = result.body}
end
```
```

}

### 5. Update Main Test Suite

Add to `test/Main.hs`:

```haskell
import qualified LuaToolboxTests

main :: IO ()
main = defaultMain $ testGroup "All Tests"
    [ OpenAPIToolboxTests.tests
    , PostgRESTParseTest.tests
    , SessionEditTests.tests
    , SessionPrintTests.tests
    , EndpointPredicateTests.tests
    , LuaToolboxTests.luaToolboxTests  -- NEW
    ]
```

### 6. Cabal Updates

Update `agents.cabal` test section:

```cabal
test-suite agents-tests
    ...
    other-modules: OpenAPIToolboxTests
                   PostgRESTParseTest
                   SessionEditTests
                   SessionPrintTests
                   EndpointPredicateTests
                   LuaToolboxTests  -- NEW
    build-depends: ...existing deps...
                  , tasty
                  , tasty-hunit
                  , temporary  -- for test file operations
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests for all Lua standard library modules
- [ ] Sandbox security tests (os.execute, io.popen blocked)
- [ ] Resource limit tests (memory, timeout)
- [ ] FS sandbox tests (path restrictions)
- [ ] HTTP sandbox tests (host restrictions)
- [ ] Portal integration tests
- [ ] Error handling tests
- [ ] Documentation guide written
- [ ] Example agent configuration provided
- [ ] All tests pass in CI
- [ ] Code coverage > 80% for LuaToolbox module

## Tradeoffs Documented

1. **Test coverage vs effort**: Focus on security-critical paths (sandbox, limits) and happy paths for standard library.
2. **Mock vs real tools**: Use mock portal for unit tests; integration tests use real tool infrastructure.
3. **Temp files for FS tests**: Use temporary directory for safe FS testing.

## Next Phase

- #278 (Final merge to main)"}

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

module LuaToolboxTests where

import Test.Tasty
import Test.Tasty.HUnit
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text

import System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox
import System.Agents.Base (LuaToolboxDescription(..))

luaToolboxTests :: TestTree
luaToolboxTests = testGroup "LuaToolbox Tests"
    [ testCase "Simple arithmetic" testSimpleArithmetic
    , testCase "Return table" testReturnTable
    , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.execute" testSandboxBlocksOsExecute
    , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.popen" testSandboxBlocksIoPopen
    , testCase "JSON module roundtrip" testJsonRoundtrip
    , testCase "Text module operations" testTextOperations
    , testCase "Time module sleep" testTimeSleep
    , testCase "FS sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
    , testCase "Memory limit enforcement" testMemoryLimit
    , testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
    , testCase "Error handling" testErrorHandling
    ]

testSimpleArithmetic :: Assertion
testSimpleArithmetic = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return 1 + 1"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 2

testReturnTable :: Assertion
testReturnTable = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return {foo = 'bar', count = 42}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["foo" .= "bar", "count" .= (42 :: Int)]

testSandboxBlocksOsExecute :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExecute = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "os.execute('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoPopen :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoPopen = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "io.popen('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion
testJsonRoundtrip = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox

        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local json = require('json')"
            , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
            , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
            , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
            , "return decoded"
            ]
    case result of

            [ "local text = require('text')"

            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = do

testMemoryLimit :: Assertion
testMemoryLimit = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 1 }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        -- Try to create a large table

    case result of

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1 }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "while true do end"
    case result of

testErrorHandling :: Assertion
testErrorHandling = do

    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do

    case result of

-- Helper functions

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox = LuaToolboxDescription
    { luaToolboxName = "test"

    "1. Query database for users",
    "2. Process results in Lua",
    "3. Write report to file",
    "4. Return summary"
  ],
  "toolDirectory": "./tools",
  "builtinToolboxes": [
    {
      "tag": "SqliteToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "memory",
        "description": "In-memory SQLite database",
        "path": ":memory:",
        "access": "read-write"
      }
    },
    {
      "tag": "LuaToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "lua",
        "description": "Sandboxed Lua interpreter for orchestration",
        "maxMemoryMB": 256,
        "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300,
        "allowedTools": ["bash", "sqlite_memory_query"],
        "allowedPaths": ["./output", "./data"],
        "allowedHosts": []
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### 5. Update Main Test Suite

Add to `test/Main.hs`:

```haskell
import qualified LuaToolboxTests

main :: IO ()
main = defaultMain $ testGroup "All Tests"
    [ OpenAPIToolboxTests.tests
    , PostgRESTParseTest.tests
    , SessionEditTests.tests
    , SessionPrintTests.tests
    , EndpointPredicateTests.tests
    , LuaToolboxTests.luaToolboxTests  -- NEW
    ]
```

### 6. Cabal Updates

Update `agents.cabal` test section:

```cabal
test-suite agents-tests
    ...
    other-modules: OpenAPIToolboxTests
                   PostgRESTParseTest
                   SessionEditTests
                   SessionPrintTests
                   EndpointPredicateTests
                   LuaToolboxTests  -- NEW
    build-depends: ...existing deps...
                  , tasty
                  , tasty-hunit
                  , temporary  -- for test file operations
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests for all Lua standard library modules
- [ ] Sandbox security tests (os.execute, io.popen blocked)
- [ ] Resource limit tests (memory, timeout)
- [ ] FS sandbox tests (path restrictions)
- [ ] HTTP sandbox tests (host restrictions)
- [ ] Portal integration tests
- [ ] Error handling tests
- [ ] Documentation guide written
- [ ] Example agent configuration provided
- [ ] All tests pass in CI
- [ ] Code coverage > 80% for LuaToolbox module

## Tradeoffs Documented

1. **Test coverage vs effort**: Focus on security-critical paths (sandbox, limits) and happy paths for standard library.
2. **Mock vs real tools**: Use mock portal for unit tests; integration tests use real tool infrastructure.
3. **Temp files for FS tests**: Use temporary directory for safe FS testing.

## Next Phase

- #278 (Final merge to main)"}

function.arguments:
{"title": "Phase 7: Tests, documentation and examples", "dependencies": "#277", "branch_name": "gh-270", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Summary

Add comprehensive tests, documentation, and working examples for the LuaToolbox feature.

## Background

This is Phase 7 of implementing LuaToolbox (#270). The feature is complete; now we add tests and documentation.

## Dependencies

- #277 (Tool registration and integration)

## Implementation

### 1. Unit Tests for LuaToolbox

Create `test/LuaToolboxTests.hs`:

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

module LuaToolboxTests where

import Test.Tasty
import Test.Tasty.HUnit
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text

import System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox
import System.Agents.Base (LuaToolboxDescription(..))

luaToolboxTests :: TestTree
luaToolboxTests = testGroup "LuaToolbox Tests"
    [ testCase "Simple arithmetic" testSimpleArithmetic
    , testCase "Return table" testReturnTable
    , testCase "Sandbox blocks os.execute" testSandboxBlocksOsExecute
    , testCase "Sandbox blocks io.popen" testSandboxBlocksIoPopen
    , testCase "JSON module roundtrip" testJsonRoundtrip
    , testCase "Text module operations" testTextOperations
    , testCase "Time module sleep" testTimeSleep
    , testCase "FS sandbox blocks unauthorized path" testFsSandbox
    , testCase "Memory limit enforcement" testMemoryLimit
    , testCase "Timeout enforcement" testTimeout
    , testCase "Error handling" testErrorHandling
    ]

testSimpleArithmetic :: Assertion
testSimpleArithmetic = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return 1 + 1"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?= Aeson.Number 2

testReturnTable :: Assertion
testReturnTable = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return {foo = 'bar', count = 42}"
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["foo" .= "bar", "count" .= (42 :: Int)]

testSandboxBlocksOsExecute :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksOsExecute = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "os.execute('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testSandboxBlocksIoPopen :: Assertion
testSandboxBlocksIoPopen = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "io.popen('echo pwned')"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with sandbox error"

testJsonRoundtrip :: Assertion
testJsonRoundtrip = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local json = require('json')"
            , "local data = {x = 1, y = 'hello', z = true}"
            , "local encoded = json.encode(data)"
            , "local decoded = json.decode(encoded)"
            , "return decoded"
            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int), "y" .= "hello", "z" .= True]

testTextOperations :: Assertion
testTextOperations = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local text = require('text')"
            , "local parts = text.split('a,b,c', ',')"
            , "return {count = #parts, first = parts[1]}"
            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult ->
            execResult.resultValue @?=
                Aeson.object ["count" .= (3 :: Int), "first" .= "a"]

testFsSandbox :: Assertion
testFsSandbox = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxAllowedPaths = ["/tmp/allowed"] }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box $ Text.unlines
            [ "local fs = require('fs')"
            , "return fs.read('/etc/passwd')"
            ]
    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult -> do
            -- Should return nil, error
            let Aeson.Object hm = execResult.resultValue
            case KeyMap.lookup "error" hm of
                Just _ -> pure ()  -- Expected error
                Nothing -> assertFailure "Should have returned error for unauthorized path"

testMemoryLimit :: Assertion
testMemoryLimit = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 1 }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        -- Try to create a large table
        executeScript box "local t = {} for i = 1, 1000000 do t[i] = i end return #t"
    case result of
        Left (MemoryError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have hit memory limit"

testTimeout :: Assertion
testTimeout = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 1 }
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "while true do end"
    case result of
        Left (TimeoutError _) -> pure ()  -- Expected
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have timed out"

testErrorHandling :: Assertion
testErrorHandling = do
    let desc = testLuaToolbox
    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScript box "return nonexistent_variable"
    case result of
        Left (LuaRuntimeError msg) ->
            assertBool "Error should mention nil value" $
                "nil" `Text.isInfixOf` msg || "attempt" `Text.isInfixOf` msg
        Left err -> assertFailure $ "Wrong error type: " ++ show err
        Right _ -> assertFailure "Should have failed with runtime error"

-- Helper functions

testLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription
testLuaToolbox = LuaToolboxDescription
    { luaToolboxName = "test"
    , luaToolboxDescription = "Test toolbox"
    , luaToolboxMaxMemoryMB = 64
    , luaToolboxMaxExecutionTimeSeconds = 5
    , luaToolboxAllowedTools = []
    , luaToolboxAllowedPaths = []
    , luaToolboxAllowedHosts = []
    }

withLuaToolbox :: LuaToolboxDescription -> (Toolbox -> IO a) -> IO (Either String a)
withLuaToolbox desc action = do
    initResult <- initializeToolbox (Tracer $ const $ pure ()) desc
    case initResult of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box -> do
            result <- action box
            closeToolbox (Tracer $ const $ pure ()) box
            pure $ Right result
```

### 2. Integration Tests with Portal

```haskell
testPortalToolCall :: Assertion
testPortalToolCall = do
    -- Create a mock portal that echoes back the call
    let mockPortal call = pure $ ToolResult
            { resultData = Aeson.object
                [ "tool" .= callToolName call
                , "args" .= callArgs call
                ]
            , resultDuration = 0.1
            , resultTraceId = "test-trace-123"
            }

    let desc = testLuaToolbox { luaToolboxAllowedTools = ["test_tool"] }

    result <- withLuaToolbox desc $ \box -> do
        executeScriptWithPortal box
            (Text.unlines
                [ "local tools = require('tools')"
                , "local result = tools.call('test_tool', {x = 1})"
                , "return result"
                ])
            (Just mockPortal)
            ["test_tool"]

    case result of
        Left err -> assertFailure $ show err
        Right execResult -> do
            let Aeson.Object hm = execResult.resultValue
            case KeyMap.lookup "data" hm >>= parseData of
                Just (tool, args) -> do
                    tool @?= "test_tool"
                    args @?= Aeson.object ["x" .= (1 :: Int)]
                Nothing -> assertFailure $ "Unexpected result: " ++ show execResult.resultValue
  where
    parseData val = case val of
        Aeson.Object d -> do
            tool <- KeyMap.lookup "tool" d >>= parseString
            args <- KeyMap.lookup "args" d
            pure (tool, args)
        _ -> Nothing
    parseString (Aeson.String s) = Just s
    parseString _ = Nothing
```

### 3. Documentation: Usage Guide

Create `docs/lua-toolbox.md`:

```markdown
# LuaToolbox Guide

The LuaToolbox provides a sandboxed Lua interpreter for agent-scriptable tool orchestration.

## Configuration

Add to your agent configuration:

```json
{
  "builtinToolboxes": [
    {
      "tag": "LuaToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "lua",
        "description": "Sandboxed Lua interpreter",
        "maxMemoryMB": 256,
        "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300,
        "allowedTools": ["bash", "sqlite"],
        "allowedPaths": ["./scripts", "./data"],
        "allowedHosts": ["localhost"]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Available Modules

### json

Encode/decode JSON:

```lua
local json = require("json")
local str = json.encode({name = "test", value = 42})
local obj = json.decode('{"x": 1}')
```

### text

UTF-8 string operations:

```lua
local text = require("text")
text.split("a,b,c", ",")  -- {"a", "b", "c"}
text.trim("  hello  ")     -- "hello"
text.startswith("hello", "he")  -- true
```

### time

Time functions:

```lua
local time = require("time")
local now = time.now()
time.sleep(2)
local str = time.format(now, "%Y-%m-%d")
```

### fs

Sandboxed filesystem access:

```lua
local fs = require("fs")
local content, err = fs.read("./data/file.txt")
fs.write("./data/output.txt", "hello")
local exists = fs.exists("./data")
```

### http

HTTP requests (host-restricted):

```lua
local http = require("http")
local result = http.get("http://localhost:3000/api")
-- result.status, result.headers, result.body
```

### tools

Call other tools through the portal:

```lua
local tools = require("tools")

-- Generic call
local result = tools.call("bash", {command = "ls -la"})

-- Convenience wrappers
local r = tools.bash.run("echo hello")
print(r.data.stdout)
```

## Security

The Lua sandbox:
- Blocks `os.execute`, `io.popen`, `loadfile`, `dofile`, `require`
- Enforces memory limits
- Enforces execution timeouts
- Restricts filesystem access to `allowedPaths`
- Restricts HTTP to `allowedHosts`
- Only allows calling whitelisted tools

## Error Handling

Use `pcall` for error handling:

```lua
local ok, result = pcall(function()
    return tools.bash.run("rm -rf /")
end)

if not ok then
    print("Error: " .. result)
end
```

## Examples

### Database Query and Process

```lua
local tools = require("tools")
local json = require("json")

-- Query database
local result = tools.sqlite.query("analytics.db", "SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10")

-- Process results
for _, row in ipairs(result.data.rows) do
    print("User: " .. row[2])
end

return {count = result.data.rowCount}
```

### HTTP API Test

```lua
local http = require("http")
local json = require("json")

local result = http.get("http://localhost:3000/health")
if result.status == 200 then
    local data = json.decode(result.body)
    return {healthy = data.status == "ok"}
else
    return {healthy = false, error = result.body}
end
```
```

### 4. Example Agent Configuration

Create `examples/lua-orchestrator-agent.json`:

```json
{
  "slug": "lua-orchestrator",
  "apiKeyId": "default",
  "flavor": "openai",
  "modelUrl": "https://api.openai.com/v1",
  "modelName": "gpt-4",
  "announce": "I can orchestrate complex workflows using Lua scripts.",
  "systemPrompt": [
    "You are a workflow orchestration agent.",
    "Use the lua_execute tool to write scripts that combine other tools.",
    "Available Lua modules: json, text, time, fs, http, tools",
    "Available tools: bash, sqlite_memory_query",
    "",
    "Example workflow:",
    "1. Query database for users",
    "2. Process results in Lua",
    "3. Write report to file",
    "4. Return summary"
  ],
  "toolDirectory": "./tools",
  "builtinToolboxes": [
    {
      "tag": "SqliteToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "memory",
        "description": "In-memory SQLite database",
        "path": ":memory:",
        "access": "read-write"
      }
    },
    {
      "tag": "LuaToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "lua",
        "description": "Sandboxed Lua interpreter for orchestration",
        "maxMemoryMB": 256,
        "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300,
        "allowedTools": ["bash", "sqlite_memory_query"],
        "allowedPaths": ["./output", "./data"],
        "allowedHosts": []
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### 5. Update Main Test Suite

Add to `test/Main.hs`:

```haskell
import qualified LuaToolboxTests

main :: IO ()
main = defaultMain $ testGroup "All Tests"
    [ OpenAPIToolboxTests.tests
    , PostgRESTParseTest.tests
    , SessionEditTests.tests
    , SessionPrintTests.tests
    , EndpointPredicateTests.tests
    , LuaToolboxTests.luaToolboxTests  -- NEW
    ]
```

### 6. Cabal Updates

Update `agents.cabal` test section:

```cabal
test-suite agents-tests
    ...
    other-modules: OpenAPIToolboxTests
                   PostgRESTParseTest
                   SessionEditTests
                   SessionPrintTests
                   EndpointPredicateTests
                   LuaToolboxTests  -- NEW
    build-depends: ...existing deps...
                  , tasty
                  , tasty-hunit
                  , temporary  -- for test file operations
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests for all Lua standard library modules
- [ ] Sandbox security tests (os.execute, io.popen blocked)
- [ ] Resource limit tests (memory, timeout)
- [ ] FS sandbox tests (path restrictions)
- [ ] HTTP sandbox tests (host restrictions)
- [ ] Portal integration tests
- [ ] Error handling tests
- [ ] Documentation guide written
- [ ] Example agent configuration provided
- [ ] All tests pass in CI
- [ ] Code coverage > 80% for LuaToolbox module

## Tradeoffs Documented

1. **Test coverage vs effort**: Focus on security-critical paths (sandbox, limits) and happy paths for standard library.
2. **Mock vs real tools**: Use mock portal for unit tests; integration tests use real tool infrastructure.
3. **Temp files for FS tests**: Use temporary directory for safe FS testing.

## Next Phase

- #278 (Final merge to main)"}

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell programmer specialized in analyzing user requirements and Haskell code.
You prepare well-documented individual issues.
You should chunk the work into smaller issues following the 'Feature Branch Isolation' model:
- Identify a 'Feature Branch' name (e.g., 'gh-123' for issue #123).
- All sub-tasks for this feature must use this branch name as their 'branch_name' argument.
- All sub-tasks should pull from and merge to this feature branch.
- Enforce linearization of work by setting 'dependencies' appropriately (e.g., task B depends on task A).
- Add a final task (the 'ultimate PR') that merges the feature branch into 'main'. This task must have 'is_final' set to 'true'.
Each individual issue you create will be executed by a human of software agent independently from each other and in the order specified by dependencies.
Your strategy should be as follows:
- avoid chunks that will generate conflicts (i.e., individual issues should not ask to modify the same file unless they are sequential)
- repeat important data types between different tasks for a same project when they are used in multiple files
You mention important tradeoffs.
You can ask user questions, when doing so always assume the answer could be 'i do not know'.
Do not create tracking issues.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.

function.arguments:
{"title": "Phase 6: Tool registration and integration with Agent runtime", "dependencies": "#276", "branch_name": "gh-270", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Summary

Integrate LuaToolbox with the agent runtime by implementing tool registration and the portal mechanism that connects Lua to other tools.

## Background

This is Phase 6 of implementing LuaToolbox (#270). Building on the hardened modules from #276, we now integrate with the existing tool infrastructure.

## Dependencies

- #276 (Security hardening and tracing)

## Implementation

### 1. Add LuaToolbox to ToolRegistration

Extend `System.Agents.ToolRegistration` to support registering LuaToolbox:

```haskell
module System.Agents.ToolRegistration where

-- ... existing imports ...
import qualified System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox as LuaTools

-- naming policy for Lua tools
lua2LLMName :: LuaTools.Toolbox -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName
lua2LLMName box toolName =
    OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat ["lua_", box.toolboxName, "_", toolName])

-- | Register a Lua toolbox with the LLM system.
--
-- Lua toolboxes expose:
-- * execute: Execute arbitrary Lua code
-- * file: Execute Lua code from a file (if allowed paths permit)
--
-- The 'execute' tool is always available. The 'file' tool is available
-- if allowedPaths is non-empty.
registerLuaTool ::
    LuaTools.Toolbox ->
    Either String ToolRegistration
registerLuaTool box =
    let toolName = "execute"
        llmName = lua2LLMName box toolName

        paramProps =
            [ ParamProperty
                { propertyKey = "script"
                , propertyType = StringParamType
                , propertyDescription = "Lua script source code to execute"
                , propertyRequired = True
                }
            , ParamProperty
                { propertyKey = "timeout"
                , propertyType = NumberParamType
                , propertyDescription = "Optional timeout override in seconds (default: toolbox configured timeout)"
                , propertyRequired = False
                }
            ]

        openaiTool =
            OpenAI.Tool
                { OpenAI.toolName = llmName
                , OpenAI.toolDescription = box.toolboxConfig.luaToolboxDescription
                , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = paramProps
                }

        -- Create the tool
        tool' = luaTool box

        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)
        find call =
            if call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName == llmName
                then Just $ mapToolResult (const call) tool'
                else Nothing
     in
        Right $ ToolRegistration
            { innerTool = mapToolResult (const ()) tool'
            , declareTool = openaiTool
            , findTool = find
            }

-- | Builder for Lua toolbox tool
luaTool :: LuaTools.Toolbox -> Tool ()
luaTool box =
    Tool
        { toolDef = LuaTool box.toolboxName
        , toolRun = run
        }
  where
    call = ()
    run tracer ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do
        -- Extract script from arguments
        case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "script") v of
            Just (Aeson.String script) -> do
                -- Get portal and allowed tools from context
                let mPortal = ctxToolPortal ctx
                let allowedTools = ctxAllowedTools ctx

                -- Execute script with portal
                result <- LuaTools.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    script
                    (contramap LuaToolsTrace tracer)
                    mPortal
                    allowedTools

                case result of
                    Left err -> pure $ LuaToolError call (Text.pack $ show err)
                    Right execResult -> pure $ LuaToolResult call (Aeson.toJSON execResult)

            _ ->
                pure $ LuaToolError call "Missing 'script' parameter or invalid type"

    run _tracer _ctx _ =
        pure $ LuaToolError call "Arguments must be a JSON object"
```

### 2. Create the Portal Implementation

Create `System.Agents.Tools.Portal` that implements the actual tool dispatch:

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}

module System.Agents.Tools.Portal where

import Control.Exception (try)
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import Data.Map.Strict (Map)
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import Data.Time (diffUTCTime, getCurrentTime)
import Data.UUID.V4 (nextRandom)
import Data.UUID (toText)

import System.Agents.Tools.Base (CallResult(..), Tool(..))
import System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolCall(..), ToolPortal, ToolResult(..), TraceId)
import qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI

-- | A registered tool that can be called through the portal
data RegisteredTool = RegisteredTool
    { regToolName :: Text
    , regToolHandler :: Aeson.Value -> IO (Either Text Aeson.Value)
    }

-- | Build a portal from a list of registered tools
--
-- This creates the callback function that Lua (or other tools) use
-- to invoke other tools.
buildPortal ::
    [RegisteredTool] ->  -- ^ Available tools
    ToolPortal
buildPortal tools call = do
    case findTool (callToolName call) tools of
        Nothing -> do
            pure $ ToolResult
                { resultData = Aeson.object ["error" .= ("Tool not found: " <> callToolName call)]
                , resultDuration = 0
                , resultTraceId = ""
                }
        Just tool -> do
            traceId <- toText <$> nextRandom
            startTime <- getCurrentTime

            result <- try $ regToolHandler tool (callArgs call)

            endTime <- getCurrentTime
            let duration = diffUTCTime endTime startTime

            case result of
                Left (e :: SomeException) ->
                    pure $ ToolResult
                        { resultData = Aeson.object ["error" .= Text.pack (show e)]
                        , resultDuration = duration
                        , resultTraceId = traceId
                        }
                Right (Left err) ->
                    pure $ ToolResult
                        { resultData = Aeson.object ["error" .= err]
                        , resultDuration = duration
                        , resultTraceId = traceId
                        }
                Right (Right val) ->
                    pure $ ToolResult
                        { resultData = val
                        , resultDuration = duration
                        , resultTraceId = traceId
                        }

findTool :: Text -> [RegisteredTool] -> Maybe RegisteredTool
findTool name = find ((== name) . regToolName)

-- | Convert a ToolRegistration to a RegisteredTool for the portal
registrationToTool :: ToolRegistration -> RegisteredTool
registrationToTool reg = RegisteredTool
    { regToolName = getToolName $ declareTool reg
    , regToolHandler = \args -> do
        -- Create a mock tool call to execute the tool
        let mockCall = OpenAI.ToolCall
                { toolCallId = OpenAI.ToolCallId "portal"
                , toolCallFunction = OpenAI.ToolCallFunction
                    { toolCallFunctionName = toolName $ declareTool reg
                    , toolCallFunctionArguments = args
                    }
                }

        case findTool mockCall reg of
            Nothing -> pure $ Left "Tool not found in registration"
            Just tool -> do
                -- Execute the tool (simplified - needs proper context)
                result <- toolRun tool (Tracer $ const $ pure ()) minimalContext args
                case result of
                    BlobToolSuccess _ bs ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.object ["output" .= Text.decodeUtf8 bs]
                    McpToolResult _ mcpResult ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.toJSON mcpResult
                    SqliteToolResult _ queryResult ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.toJSON queryResult
                    SystemToolResult _ sysResult ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.toJSON sysResult
                    -- Handle other result types...
                    err ->
                        pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ show err
    }
  where
    minimalContext = mkMinimalContext
        (SessionId $ error "mock session")
        (ConversationId $ error "mock conversation")
        (TurnId $ error "mock turn")

getToolName :: OpenAI.Tool -> Text
getToolName tool = case toolName tool of
    OpenAI.ToolName t -> t
```

### 3. Update Runtime to Support LuaToolbox Initialization

Modify `System.Agents.Runtime` (or appropriate runtime module) to initialize LuaToolbox:

```haskell
-- In the toolbox initialization section
initializeBuiltinToolbox ::
    Tracer IO RuntimeTrace ->
    BuiltinToolboxDescription ->
    IO (Either String [ToolRegistration])
initializeBuiltinToolbox tracer (SqliteToolbox desc) = do
    result <- SqliteTools.initializeToolbox (contramap SqliteTrace tracer) desc
    case result of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box -> SqliteTools.registerSqliteTools box

initializeBuiltinToolbox tracer (SystemToolbox desc) = do
    result <- SystemTools.initializeToolbox (contramap SystemTrace tracer) desc
    case result of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box -> SystemTools.registerSystemTools box

initializeBuiltinToolbox tracer (LuaToolbox desc) = do  -- NEW
    result <- LuaTools.initializeToolbox (contramap LuaTrace tracer) desc
    case result of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box ->
            case registerLuaTool box of
                Left err -> pure $ Left err
                Right reg -> pure $ Right [reg]
```

### 4. Update Agent Configuration

The `Agent` type in `System.Agents.Base` already has `builtinToolboxes :: Maybe [BuiltinToolboxDescription]`. The `LuaToolbox` constructor added in #272 enables:

```json
{
  "builtinToolboxes": [
    {
      "tag": "LuaToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "lua",
        "description": "Sandboxed Lua interpreter for tool orchestration",
        "maxMemoryMB": 256,
        "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300,
        "allowedTools": ["bash", "sqlite", "io"],
        "allowedPaths": ["./scripts", "./data"],
        "allowedHosts": ["localhost", "127.0.0.1"]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### 5. Extend Runtime Trace to Include LuaToolbox

```haskell
data RuntimeTrace
    = SqliteTrace SqliteTools.Trace
    | SystemTrace SystemTools.Trace
    | LuaTrace LuaTools.Trace  -- NEW
    | ...
```

## Integration Flow

```
Agent Configuration
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Runtime initializes LuaToolbox            |
| - Create Lua state                        |
| - Configure sandbox                       |
| - Register stdlib modules                 |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| ToolRegistration creates lua_execute tool |
| - Tool declares to LLM                    |
| - ToolRun receives ToolExecutionContext   |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| LLM calls lua_execute with script         |
| ToolRun calls executeScriptWithPortal     |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Lua script runs:                          |
| local tools = require("tools")            |
| tools.bash.run("ls -la")                  |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| tools.call uses ToolPortal from context   |
| Portal dispatches to registered tool      |
| Result returns to Lua                     |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Lua script returns JSON                   |
| Result sent to LLM                        |
+-------------------------------------------+
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `registerLuaTool` creates proper tool registration
- [ ] `luaTool` builder executes Lua scripts with portal support
- [ ] Portal correctly dispatches to registered tools by name
- [ ] Tool results flow back through portal to Lua
- [ ] Errors in tool calls are properly caught and returned
- [ ] Runtime initializes LuaToolbox from configuration
- [ ] LuaToolbox integrates with existing agent runtime
- [ ] Full round-trip works: agent -> lua -> bash -> result -> agent
- [ ] Multiple Lua toolboxes can coexist (different names)
- [ ] Code compiles and runs without runtime errors

## Tradeoffs Documented

1. **Synchronous portal implementation**: Tool calls block Lua execution. This is simpler and matches the design spec.
2. **Portal recreated per execution**: The portal is built fresh each time. Could be cached for performance, but recreation ensures fresh state.
3. **Tool name matching**: Uses string names for lookup. Could use more type-safe approach, but string names match how tools are declared.

## Next Phase

- #277 (Tests and documentation)"}

{"title": "Phase 6: Tool registration and integration with Agent runtime", "dependencies": "#276", "branch_name": "gh-270", "is_final": "false", "body": "## Summary

Integrate LuaToolbox with the agent runtime by implementing tool registration and the portal mechanism that connects Lua to other tools.

## Background

This is Phase 6 of implementing LuaToolbox (#270). Building on the hardened modules from #276, we now integrate with the existing tool infrastructure.

## Dependencies

- #276 (Security hardening and tracing)

## Implementation

### 1. Add LuaToolbox to ToolRegistration

Extend `System.Agents.ToolRegistration` to support registering LuaToolbox:

```haskell
module System.Agents.ToolRegistration where

-- ... existing imports ...
import qualified System.Agents.Tools.LuaToolbox as LuaTools

-- naming policy for Lua tools
lua2LLMName :: LuaTools.Toolbox -> Text -> OpenAI.ToolName
lua2LLMName box toolName =
    OpenAI.ToolName (mconcat ["lua_", box.toolboxName, "_", toolName])

-- | Register a Lua toolbox with the LLM system.
--
-- Lua toolboxes expose:
-- * execute: Execute arbitrary Lua code
-- * file: Execute Lua code from a file (if allowed paths permit)
--
-- The 'execute' tool is always available. The 'file' tool is available
-- if allowedPaths is non-empty.
registerLuaTool ::
    LuaTools.Toolbox ->
    Either String ToolRegistration
registerLuaTool box =
    let toolName = "execute"
        llmName = lua2LLMName box toolName

        paramProps =
            [ ParamProperty
                { propertyKey = "script"
                , propertyType = StringParamType
                , propertyDescription = "Lua script source code to execute"
                , propertyRequired = True
                }
            , ParamProperty
                { propertyKey = "timeout"
                , propertyType = NumberParamType
                , propertyDescription = "Optional timeout override in seconds (default: toolbox configured timeout)"
                , propertyRequired = False
                }
            ]

        openaiTool =
            OpenAI.Tool
                { OpenAI.toolName = llmName
                , OpenAI.toolDescription = box.toolboxConfig.luaToolboxDescription
                , OpenAI.toolParamProperties = paramProps
                }

        -- Create the tool
        tool' = luaTool box

        find :: OpenAI.ToolCall -> Maybe (Tool OpenAI.ToolCall)
        find call =
            if call.toolCallFunction.toolCallFunctionName == llmName
                then Just $ mapToolResult (const call) tool'
                else Nothing
     in
        Right $ ToolRegistration
            { innerTool = mapToolResult (const ()) tool'
            , declareTool = openaiTool
            , findTool = find
            }

-- | Builder for Lua toolbox tool
luaTool :: LuaTools.Toolbox -> Tool ()
luaTool box =
    Tool
        { toolDef = LuaTool box.toolboxName
        , toolRun = run
        }
  where
    call = ()
    run tracer ctx (Aeson.Object v) = do
        -- Extract script from arguments
        case KeyMap.lookup (AesonKey.fromText "script") v of
            Just (Aeson.String script) -> do
                -- Get portal and allowed tools from context
                let mPortal = ctxToolPortal ctx
                let allowedTools = ctxAllowedTools ctx

                -- Execute script with portal
                result <- LuaTools.executeScriptWithPortal
                    box
                    script
                    (contramap LuaToolsTrace tracer)
                    mPortal
                    allowedTools

                case result of
                    Left err -> pure $ LuaToolError call (Text.pack $ show err)
                    Right execResult -> pure $ LuaToolResult call (Aeson.toJSON execResult)

            _ ->
                pure $ LuaToolError call "Missing 'script' parameter or invalid type"

    run _tracer _ctx _ =
        pure $ LuaToolError call "Arguments must be a JSON object"
```

### 2. Create the Portal Implementation

Create `System.Agents.Tools.Portal` that implements the actual tool dispatch:

```haskell
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}

module System.Agents.Tools.Portal where

import Control.Exception (try)
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import Data.Map.Strict (Map)
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import Data.Time (diffUTCTime, getCurrentTime)
import Data.UUID.V4 (nextRandom)
import Data.UUID (toText)

import System.Agents.Tools.Base (CallResult(..), Tool(..))
import System.Agents.Tools.Context (ToolCall(..), ToolPortal, ToolResult(..), TraceId)
import qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI

-- | A registered tool that can be called through the portal
data RegisteredTool = RegisteredTool
    { regToolName :: Text
    , regToolHandler :: Aeson.Value -> IO (Either Text Aeson.Value)
    }

-- | Build a portal from a list of registered tools
--
-- This creates the callback function that Lua (or other tools) use
-- to invoke other tools.
buildPortal ::
    [RegisteredTool] ->  -- ^ Available tools
    ToolPortal
buildPortal tools call = do
    case findTool (callToolName call) tools of
        Nothing -> do
            pure $ ToolResult
                { resultData = Aeson.object ["error" .= ("Tool not found: " <> callToolName call)]
                , resultDuration = 0
                , resultTraceId = ""
                }
        Just tool -> do
            traceId <- toText <$> nextRandom
            startTime <- getCurrentTime

            result <- try $ regToolHandler tool (callArgs call)

            endTime <- getCurrentTime
            let duration = diffUTCTime endTime startTime

            case result of
                Left (e :: SomeException) ->
                    pure $ ToolResult
                        { resultData = Aeson.object ["error" .= Text.pack (show e)]
                        , resultDuration = duration
                        , resultTraceId = traceId
                        }
                Right (Left err) ->
                    pure $ ToolResult
                        { resultData = Aeson.object ["error" .= err]
                        , resultDuration = duration
                        , resultTraceId = traceId
                        }
                Right (Right val) ->
                    pure $ ToolResult
                        { resultData = val
                        , resultDuration = duration
                        , resultTraceId = traceId
                        }

findTool :: Text -> [RegisteredTool] -> Maybe RegisteredTool
findTool name = find ((== name) . regToolName)

-- | Convert a ToolRegistration to a RegisteredTool for the portal
registrationToTool :: ToolRegistration -> RegisteredTool
registrationToTool reg = RegisteredTool
    { regToolName = getToolName $ declareTool reg
    , regToolHandler = \args -> do
        -- Create a mock tool call to execute the tool
        let mockCall = OpenAI.ToolCall
                { toolCallId = OpenAI.ToolCallId "portal"
                , toolCallFunction = OpenAI.ToolCallFunction
                    { toolCallFunctionName = toolName $ declareTool reg
                    , toolCallFunctionArguments = args
                    }
                }

        case findTool mockCall reg of
            Nothing -> pure $ Left "Tool not found in registration"
            Just tool -> do
                -- Execute the tool (simplified - needs proper context)
                result <- toolRun tool (Tracer $ const $ pure ()) minimalContext args
                case result of
                    BlobToolSuccess _ bs ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.object ["output" .= Text.decodeUtf8 bs]
                    McpToolResult _ mcpResult ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.toJSON mcpResult
                    SqliteToolResult _ queryResult ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.toJSON queryResult
                    SystemToolResult _ sysResult ->
                        pure $ Right $ Aeson.toJSON sysResult
                    -- Handle other result types...
                    err ->
                        pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ show err
    }
  where
    minimalContext = mkMinimalContext
        (SessionId $ error "mock session")
        (ConversationId $ error "mock conversation")
        (TurnId $ error "mock turn")

getToolName :: OpenAI.Tool -> Text
getToolName tool = case toolName tool of
    OpenAI.ToolName t -> t
```

### 3. Update Runtime to Support LuaToolbox Initialization

Modify `System.Agents.Runtime` (or appropriate runtime module) to initialize LuaToolbox:

```haskell
-- In the toolbox initialization section
initializeBuiltinToolbox ::
    Tracer IO RuntimeTrace ->
    BuiltinToolboxDescription ->
    IO (Either String [ToolRegistration])
initializeBuiltinToolbox tracer (SqliteToolbox desc) = do
    result <- SqliteTools.initializeToolbox (contramap SqliteTrace tracer) desc
    case result of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box -> SqliteTools.registerSqliteTools box

initializeBuiltinToolbox tracer (SystemToolbox desc) = do
    result <- SystemTools.initializeToolbox (contramap SystemTrace tracer) desc
    case result of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box -> SystemTools.registerSystemTools box

initializeBuiltinToolbox tracer (LuaToolbox desc) = do  -- NEW
    result <- LuaTools.initializeToolbox (contramap LuaTrace tracer) desc
    case result of
        Left err -> pure $ Left err
        Right box ->
            case registerLuaTool box of
                Left err -> pure $ Left err
                Right reg -> pure $ Right [reg]
```

### 4. Update Agent Configuration

The `Agent` type in `System.Agents.Base` already has `builtinToolboxes :: Maybe [BuiltinToolboxDescription]`. The `LuaToolbox` constructor added in #272 enables:

```json
{
  "builtinToolboxes": [
    {
      "tag": "LuaToolbox",
      "contents": {
        "name": "lua",
        "description": "Sandboxed Lua interpreter for tool orchestration",
        "maxMemoryMB": 256,
        "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300,
        "allowedTools": ["bash", "sqlite", "io"],
        "allowedPaths": ["./scripts", "./data"],
        "allowedHosts": ["localhost", "127.0.0.1"]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### 5. Extend Runtime Trace to Include LuaToolbox

```haskell
data RuntimeTrace
    = SqliteTrace SqliteTools.Trace
    | SystemTrace SystemTools.Trace
    | LuaTrace LuaTools.Trace  -- NEW
    | ...
```

## Integration Flow

```
Agent Configuration
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Runtime initializes LuaToolbox            |
| - Create Lua state                        |
| - Configure sandbox                       |
| - Register stdlib modules                 |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| ToolRegistration creates lua_execute tool |
| - Tool declares to LLM                    |
| - ToolRun receives ToolExecutionContext   |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| LLM calls lua_execute with script         |
| ToolRun calls executeScriptWithPortal     |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Lua script runs:                          |
| local tools = require("tools")            |
| tools.bash.run("ls -la")                  |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| tools.call uses ToolPortal from context   |
| Portal dispatches to registered tool      |
| Result returns to Lua                     |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Lua script returns JSON                   |
| Result sent to LLM                        |
+-------------------------------------------+
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `registerLuaTool` creates proper tool registration
- [ ] `luaTool` builder executes Lua scripts with portal support
- [ ] Portal correctly dispatches to registered tools by name
- [ ] Tool results flow back through portal to Lua
- [ ] Errors in tool calls are properly caught and returned
- [ ] Runtime initializes LuaToolbox from configuration
- [ ] LuaToolbox integrates with existing agent runtime
- [ ] Full round-trip works: agent -> lua -> bash -> result -> agent
- [ ] Multiple Lua toolboxes can coexist (different names)
- [ ] Code compiles and runs without runtime errors

## Tradeoffs Documented

1. **Synchronous portal implementation**: Tool calls block Lua execution. This is simpler and matches the design spec.
2. **Portal recreated per execution**: The portal is built fresh each time. Could be cached for performance, but recreation ensures fresh state.
3. **Tool name matching**: Uses string names for lookup. Could use more type-safe approach, but string names match how tools are declared.

## Next Phase

- #277 (Tests and documentation)"}

findTool :: Text -> [RegisteredTool] -> Maybe RegisteredTool

-- | Convert a ToolRegistration to a RegisteredTool for the portal
registrationToTool :: ToolRegistration -> RegisteredTool
registrationToTool reg = RegisteredTool

    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| ToolRegistration creates lua_execute tool |
| - Tool declares to LLM                    |
| - ToolRun receives ToolExecutionContext   |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| LLM calls lua_execute with script         |
| ToolRun calls executeScriptWithPortal     |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Lua script runs:                          |
| local tools = require("tools")            |
| tools.bash.run("ls -la")                  |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| tools.call uses ToolPortal from context   |
| Portal dispatches to registered tool      |
| Result returns to Lua                     |
+-------------------------------------------+
    |
    v
+-------------------------------------------+
| Lua script returns JSON                   |
| Result sent to LLM                        |
+-------------------------------------------+
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `registerLuaTool` creates proper tool registration
- [ ] `luaTool` builder executes Lua scripts with portal support
- [ ] Portal correctly dispatches to registered tools by name
- [ ] Tool results flow back through portal to Lua
- [ ] Errors in tool calls are properly caught and returned
- [ ] Runtime initializes LuaToolbox from configuration
- [ ] LuaToolbox integrates with existing agent runtime
- [ ] Full round-trip works: agent -> lua -> bash -> result -> agent
- [ ] Multiple Lua toolboxes can coexist (different names)
- [ ] Code compiles and runs without runtime errors

## Tradeoffs Documented

1. **Synchronous portal implementation**: Tool calls block Lua execution. This is simpler and matches the design spec.
2. **Portal recreated per execution**: The portal is built fresh each time. Could be cached for performance, but recreation ensures fresh state.
3. **Tool name matching**: Uses string names for lookup. Could use more type-safe approach, but string names match how tools are declared.

## Next Phase

- #277 (Tests and documentation)"}
